description:
Advances: spec.system, specled.package

Deliverable:
1. Ceremony deletion: .spec/AGENTS.md "take either side and regenerate" ritual (lines ~34-38) DELETED (not rewritten — pinned); docs/concepts.md committed-state framing (lines ~23, 128, 191, 219, 429, 462) rewritten to the evidence-store model; docs/adoption.md severity-table state.json reference updated + CI setup section gains the read-only fetch line (`git fetch origin +refs/heads/spec-evidence:refs/remotes/origin/spec-evidence || true`) with the trust caveat: evidence is an unauthenticated attestation, forbidden as merge-gate/pass-fail input.
2. Repo-shipped skill + bootstrap references (specled_ex repo content, not user personal skills — in scope): skills/spec-led-bootstrap/SKILL.md + references/{detection-matrix,adoption-phases,configurable-levers,task-templates}.md — replace "commit state.json baseline" guidance with migrate/sync/hook flow.
3. priv/spec_init templates: README.md.eex, AGENTS.md.eex, agents/skills/spec-led-development/SKILL.md.eex, specs/spec_system.spec.md.eex, workflows/spec_review.yml.eex (CI workflow template gets the fetch line — "part of documented CI setup / bootstrap output").
4. Theoretical tooling-integration guidance (docs only, pinned): one paragraph in docs/adoption.md noting workflow tooling MAY call mix spec.sync at natural push points and that tooling-then-hook double sync is a no-op by construction. NO skill edits outside this repo.
5. Prose sweep of .spec/specs/*.spec.md narrative-only state.json mentions (spec_system, package, modal_class, binding, triangulation, policy_files, use_tier per grep) — prose corrections only, no requirement semantics changes (append-only guard enforces). README.md + CHANGELOG.md entry.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: full merge-gate suite; existing spec.init task tests updated in place if any assert template content.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: grep-clean assertion that no shipped doc/template still instructs committing or hand-merging state.json; CI template contains the fetch line + trust caveat.
- Verification cadence:
  - Iteration tier: compile-graph-scoped checks only plus mix spec.check --no-run-commands.
  - Final tier: full merge-gate suite run-until-green before IMPLEMENTED (spec.check prose/policy-file mapping may raise co-change findings; adjudicate against the S1 baseline note).
  - Verifier tier: flipped stubs + targeted/--stale only; no full suite.
- Standing bounce-class checks:
  - No new callable surface expected — if one appears, that is scope creep, bounce it
  - Spec surfaces fresh for every touched config surface
  - Prose-only guarantee: append-only guard green proves no requirement semantics changed
- Call-site Integration: N/A — docs/templates only, no new callable surface; gate is the full merge-gate suite plus the grep-clean assertion above.

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/** (all code stages done)
- test/** except spec.init template-content tests
- .spec/state.json (untracked as of S5 — must NOT reappear in the index)
Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- README.md
- CHANGELOG.md
- docs/concepts.md
- docs/adoption.md
- .spec/AGENTS.md
- skills/spec-led-bootstrap/SKILL.md
- skills/spec-led-bootstrap/references/*.md
- priv/spec_init/README.md.eex
- priv/spec_init/AGENTS.md.eex
- priv/spec_init/agents/skills/spec-led-development/SKILL.md.eex
- priv/spec_init/specs/spec_system.spec.md.eex
- priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex
- .spec/specs/*.spec.md (prose-only sweep — append-only guard enforces no semantics changes)
- test/mix/tasks/spec_init_test.exs (template-content assertions, if present)
- .spec/realization_hashes.json (tool-managed regen)

Out of Scope (intent):
- No spec.init behavior changes (adopter bootstrap experience is a recorded future direction, out of scope this arc) — template TEXT only.
- No code changes outside .eex templates; if the prose guard or file-touch guard demands a lib change, that is a BOUNCE back to planning, not silent scope growth.
- Do not carry the "take either side and regenerate" guidance into any new doc.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] Tests listed above pass
- [ ] Spec verification passes (mix spec.check; adjudicate against S1 baseline note)
- [ ] mix format --check-formatted; mix deps.unlock --check-unused
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only Allowed globs (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 0, passed: 0 (docs/templates/spec-prose ticket -- no flipped stubs, as expected)
- Requirements coverage (docs-gate equivalents):

Independent grep over docs/**, README.md, .spec/AGENTS.md, skills/spec-led-bootstrap/**, priv/spec_init/** finds NO remaining instruction to commit / hand-merge / "take either side and regenerate" .spec/state.json. Only hit is README.md:284, which is the correct new "Do not use .spec/state.json as source-of-truth" caveat.
  - .spec/AGENTS.md "take either side and regenerate" ritual: DELETED (not rewritten) -- the bullet was removed and replaced with derived-diagnostics guidance (mix spec.index/validate --output).
  - docs/adoption.md CI fetch line: PRESENT (git fetch origin +refs/heads/spec-evidence:refs/remotes/origin/spec-evidence || true) WITH trust caveat (evidence is an unauthenticated attestation, forbidden as merge-gate/pass-fail input).
  - priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex fetch line: PRESENT with the same read-only/unauthenticated-attestation caveat comment.
  - tooling-integration paragraph: PRESENT in docs/adoption.md (tooling MAY call mix spec.sync at push points; tooling-then-hook double sync is a no-op by construction).
  - Two untouched-but-named templates: priv/spec_init/AGENTS.md.eex has NO state.json mention (genuinely nothing to change); priv/spec_init/specs/spec_system.spec.md.eex carries only the accurate "indexing/validation generates .spec/state.json" requirement, no commit/merge ceremony (genuinely nothing to change). Neither is a gap.
  - Named subjects left unswept (modal_class, binding, triangulation, policy_files, use_tier): their state.json mentions are all accurate current-truth ("never serialized into state.json", "shall not mutate state.json", "not persisted to state.json", "HashStore shall not persist the baseline inside state.json", legacy-fallback scenarios). None instruct commit/merge; correctly left alone.
  - mix spec.check (plain, stage worktree): status=pass errors=0 warnings=0. The orchestrator's adjudicated warning spec.system surface_target_missing .spec/state.json is RESOLVED (the .spec/state.json surface entry was removed from spec_system.spec.md). Single branch finding is an info-level co-change line, acceptable.
- Intent-level scope concerns: TWO additive requirement-statement semantic changes in the .spec.md sweep (see Gaps). The ticket scopes .spec/specs/*.spec.md to "prose corrections only, no requirement semantics changes"; these are undisclosed additive tightenings that the append-only guard structurally cannot catch (it fires on deletions/downgrades/weakenings, not additive expansions), so the ticket's "append-only guard green proves no requirement semantics changed" safety theory does not clear them.
- Targeted verification: PASS on the gates run -- mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; mix format --check-formatted clean; mix deps.unlock --check-unused clean; mix test test/mix/tasks/spec_tasks_test.exs = 27 tests, 0 failures (template-content assertions are template-vs-scaffold equality, self-consistent under template edits).
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design)
- Audit-expanded scope: none (no prior AUDIT comments on this ticket)

Verdict: FAIL

Gaps:
1. .spec/specs/next.spec.md -- requirement specled.next.read_only (priority: must, stability: evolving): statement changed from "...without writing current-truth files or derived state." to "...without writing current-truth files, derived local state, or evidence refs." The base statement never mentioned state.json, so this is NOT a state.json prose sweep -- it is a new prohibition (evidence refs) added to a must-requirement, i.e. a requirement-semantics amendment. Out of scope for this prose-only stage; also undisclosed in the IMPLEMENTED comment (which claims only "surfaces/prose").
2. .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md -- requirement specled.tasks.init_local_skill (priority: should, stability: evolving): statement gained the normative clause "...including guidance that treats .spec/state.json as derived local state rather than shared source-of-truth data." The base statement never mentioned state.json; this adds a new content obligation on the scaffolded skill, tracking this stage's template edit. Requirement-semantics amendment, out of scope for the prose-only sweep, undisclosed.

Remediation: revert the statement fields of specled.next.read_only and specled.tasks.init_local_skill back to their base text (7b4ab82). If the tightening is genuinely wanted, it belongs in a follow-up spec-maintenance ticket with explicit append-only consideration, not this docs/ceremony stage. Everything else in the diff is a clean, on-target prose/surface sweep.

Notes (advisory):
- Both gap edits are behavior-accurate current-truth updates and low-risk; the objection is scope discipline, not correctness. Remediation is a mechanical 2-hunk revert.
- The index_state.spec.md scenario.conflict_ritual_preserves_baseline given/then reframe (merge-conflict "take either side" -> "removed because derived state is regenerated locally") is NOT counted as a gap: the tested invariant (baseline preserved on regen) is unchanged and the reframe removes exactly the deleted ceremony -- that is legitimate sweep work.
- package.spec.md and spec_review.spec.md surface additions (bootstrap skill/refs and init templates) are the expected "spec surfaces fresh for every touched config surface" updates; correct.

AUDIT: BOUNCE
Session: 8384310d-16ba-4102-b635-60dea30661d7
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/8384310d-16ba-4102-b635-60dea30661d7.jsonl

Reasoning: The verifier's FAIL is correct and its gap list is complete. I independently diffed both flagged statements against base 7b4ab82: next.spec.md specled.next.read_only (must) gained "or evidence refs" — a new prohibition on a must-requirement, and its base never mentioned state.json so it is not a sweep correction; mix_tasks.spec.md specled.tasks.init_local_skill (should) gained a new normative clause obliging the scaffolded skill to carry state.json-as-derived guidance. Both are additive tightenings the append-only guard structurally cannot catch, and both files are outside the deliverable-5 sweep subject list (spec_system, package, modal_class, binding, triangulation, policy_files, use_tier). I swept the remaining .spec.md hunks myself and found no other requirement/scenario semantics change the verifier missed: the index_state conflict_ritual_preserves_baseline reframe keeps the when/then invariant intact while dropping exactly the deleted "take either side" ceremony (legitimate); package/spec_review surface additions and the spec_system .spec/state.json surface removal are surface-list updates, not statements. Spot-checks confirm the clean findings — git ls-files .spec/state.json empty, grep-clean (sole hit README.md:284 is the correct new caveat), AGENTS.md ritual deleted not rewritten, and the fetch line + unauthenticated-attestation caveat present in both docs/adoption.md and priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex.

BOUNCE -> implementer: revert the two statement fields to their base 7b4ab82 text.
  1. .spec/specs/next.spec.md specled.next.read_only statement -> "mix spec.next shall stay read-only and return guidance output without writing current-truth files or derived state."
  2. .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md specled.tasks.init_local_skill statement -> "In interactive runs, mix spec.init shall offer to scaffold a local Skill for Spec Led Development and write it when the prompt is accepted."
If either tightening is genuinely wanted, it belongs in a follow-up spec-maintenance ticket with explicit append-only consideration, not this docs/ceremony stage. Everything else in the diff is clean, on-target prose/surface work; no BOUNCE -> verifier.

VERIFY: FAIL
Session: 8384310d-16ba-4102-b635-60dea30661d7
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-src-specled-ex/8384310d-16ba-4102-b635-60dea30661d7.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 17 cumulative vs base 7b4ab82 (remediation 8beb7f9..908b3eb touched ONLY .spec/specs/next.spec.md and .spec/specs/mix_tasks.spec.md, confirmed)
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0 (remediation added no new files; scope unchanged from prior audit-confirmed 0)
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: none (docs ticket)
- Requirements coverage:
  - Reverted statements verbatim-match base: YES. specled.next.read_only and specled.tasks.init_local_skill both now byte-identical to base (git diff 7b4ab82..HEAD for both files is EMPTY; neither file appears in the cumulative-vs-base name-only diff). The two forbidden statement tightenings were the ONLY edits those files carried, so the full revert is correct and the legitimate prose sweep remains in the other 17 files.
  - grep-clean: YES. No commit/hand-merge/take-either-side/"commit state.json" ceremony across docs/** README.md .spec/AGENTS.md skills/spec-led-bootstrap/** priv/spec_init/**. All state.json mentions are the legitimate "derived local state, generate on request, not source-of-truth" framing (AGENTS.md:34-36, README.md:276/284, README.md.eex:16, SKILL.md.eex:107/115). AGENTS.md ritual still deleted (only a descriptive "Generated vs Committed State" section remains).
  - fetch line + caveat: YES. Evidence fetch present in docs/adoption.md:149 and priv/spec_init/workflows/spec_review.yml.eex:66; unauthenticated-attestation caveat present in adoption.md:156 and spec_review.yml.eex:64.
  - spec.check clean: NO -- see gap below.
  - state.json not in index: YES (git ls-files .spec/state.json empty).
- Intent-level scope concerns: none
- Targeted verification: mix compile --warnings-as-errors PASS; mix format --check-formatted PASS; mix spec.check FAIL (exit 1).
- Full-suite verification: not run by verifier (by design)
- Audit-expanded scope: none

Verdict: FAIL

Gaps (if FAIL):
- `mix spec.check` exits non-zero. Authored line is clean (status=pass errors=0 warnings=0, subjects=34 requirements=350), BUT the branch section reports findings=2 including:
    [ERROR] branch_guard_missing_subject_update priv/spec_init/README.md.eex :: Changed file priv/spec_init/README.md.eex impacts subject specs that were not updated: specled.next
  and fails with "** (Mix) Spec check failed: 2 branch finding(s)". This is an ERROR-severity branch finding, not the "cross_cutting info" carve-out.
- Root cause / attribution: this is a direct, mechanical consequence of the prescribed revert. This ticket's own docs sweep edited priv/spec_init/README.md.eex (mapped to subject specled.next). Branch_guard therefore requires next.spec.md to be updated relative to origin/main. Pre-remediation (8beb7f9), next.spec.md WAS changed vs origin/main -> guard satisfied -> spec.check clean. The audit-prescribed revert returned next.spec.md to base (== origin/main for this file), removing the only update that satisfied the guard. Verified: git diff origin/main..8beb7f9 lists next.spec.md; git diff origin/main..HEAD does NOT.
- This is a genuine tension between the prose-only-sweep contract (forbids touching next.spec.md's statement) and branch_guard (requires next.spec.md updated because same-ticket README.md.eex changed). The implementer followed the audit prescription exactly; the prescription itself has this side effect. Resolution needs adjudication, e.g.: (a) a permitted NON-statement prose update to next.spec.md that re-satisfies branch_guard without re-tightening the requirement, or (b) handling/re-scoping the README.md.eex hunk mapped to specled.next, or (c) an explicit branch_guard waiver for this docs ticket.

Notes (advisory only):
- Everything else in the narrow pass is green: remediation surgically correct (2 files, both fully reverted to base), all core docs deliverables intact and undisturbed, state.json untracked, compile + format clean.
- The `branch change_type=cross_cutting` line and impacted_subjects list are the acceptable info-level output; the blocking item is specifically the branch_guard_missing_subject_update ERROR.
- I did not run the full suite (per instructions). I did not fix anything and did not close the ticket.

ORCHESTRATOR ADJUDICATION (re: VERIFY FAIL 2026-07-17, branch_guard_missing_subject_update priv/spec_init/README.md.eex -> specled.next): Option (a) authorized — a minimal, truthful, NON-statement prose touch to .spec/specs/next.spec.md that re-satisfies the guard without changing any requirement/scenario semantics. Rationale: the guard error is a direct side effect of the audit-prescribed revert (next.spec.md became byte-identical to origin/main while this ticket's README.md.eex edit maps to specled.next); same guard-forced-minimal-co-change class as the two prior orchestrator-authorized gate fixes on this epic (.1 format, .2 subject co-updates). next.spec.md prose is within this ticket's Allowed .spec/specs/*.spec.md sweep; the prose-only contract forbids semantics changes, not truthful prose. Does NOT consume a bounce attempt (audit-induced, not implementer error). Statement fields must remain byte-identical to base.
